req.title: Recurring transfer (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.
preconditions: tier in {standard,premium}

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three distinct axes: (1) tier-based per-occurrence limits (standard <= $2,500, premium <= $10,000), (2) idempotency on duplicate Idempotency-Key submissions, and (3) error paths for limit violations and unsupported frequencies. I derived test cases using EP to cover valid tier partitions, BVA to probe the exact cap boundaries, NEG for over-limit and bad-frequency submissions, DT to capture the cross-product of tier x amount decisions, and STC to verify the idempotency state transition (initial -> persisted -> duplicate returns same). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down their own customers and tokens. All tests carry explicit traces to R-FIN-080, all positive tests have non-empty preconditions, and the suite contains at least one negative and one boundary case.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit
preconditions: Authenticated premium-tier customer exists with tier=premium
Source account has sufficient funds (balance > $5,000)
WEEKLY frequency is supported by the system
A unique Idempotency-Key has been generated for this request
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with body {amount:5000, currency:USD, frequency:WEEKLY, source_account:<id>, destination_account:<id>} and headers Authorization: Bearer <premium_token> and Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Capture HTTP response status code and response body
expected: Response body contains field next_run with a future ISO-8601 timestamp
trace: R-FIN-080: premium-tier customer may schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; $5,000 is within that limit
R-FIN-080: expected outcome - next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit(api_client, premium_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account": "<source_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<destination_account_id>"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run" in body

tests.name: test_standard_tier_amount_exceeds_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated standard-tier customer exists with tier=standard
Amount requested (2501) exceeds the standard per-occurrence cap of $2,500
A unique Idempotency-Key has been generated for this request
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with body {amount:2501, currency:USD, frequency:WEEKLY, source_account:<id>, destination_account:<id>} and headers Authorization: Bearer <standard_token> and Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Capture HTTP response status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an error or detail field referencing the per-occurrence limit
trace: R-FIN-080: non-premium accounts are capped at $2,500 per occurrence; $2,501 exceeds that cap
R-FIN-080: error pathway - 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_standard_tier_amount_exceeds_cap_returns_422(api_client, standard_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account": "<source_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<destination_account_id>"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

tests.name: test_standard_tier_at_exact_cap_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated standard-tier customer exists with tier=standard
Source account has sufficient funds (balance >= $2,500)
Amount equals exactly the standard per-occurrence cap of $2,500
WEEKLY frequency is supported by the system
A unique Idempotency-Key has been generated for this request
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with body {amount:2500, currency:USD, frequency:WEEKLY, source_account:<id>, destination_account:<id>} and headers Authorization: Bearer <standard_token> and Idempotency-Key: <uuid>

expected: Response body contains field next_run with a future ISO-8601 timestamp
trace: R-FIN-080: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500; the boundary value $2,500 must be accepted (inclusive upper bound)
R-FIN-080: expected outcome - next run calculated

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: Authenticated premium-tier customer exists with tier=premium
Source account has sufficient funds (balance > $5,000)
WEEKLY frequency is supported by the system
A fixed Idempotency-Key value has been chosen for both submissions
First submission has already been successfully processed and returned HTTP 201
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with body {amount:5000, currency:USD, frequency:WEEKLY, source_account:<id>, destination_account:<id>} and headers Authorization: Bearer <premium_token> and Idempotency-Key: <fixed_uuid> (first call)
Record schedule_id and next_run from first response
POST /transfers/recurring with identical body and identical Idempotency-Key header (second call)
Capture HTTP response status code and response body from second call
expected: Second call returns HTTP 200 or 201 (same success class as first call)
schedule_id in second response matches schedule_id from first response
next_run in second response matches next_run from first response
No duplicate schedule record is created in the system
trace: R-FIN-080: the operation must be idempotent - duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result
R-FIN-080: expected outcome - schedule persisted (only once)
R-FIN-080: expected outcome - next run calculated (same value returned)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(api_client, premium_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account": "<source_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<destination_account_id>"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    first_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first_resp.status_code == 201
    first_body = first_resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in first_body
    assert "next_run" in first_body

    second_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    second_body = second_resp.json()
    assert second_body["schedule_id"] == first_body["schedule_id"]
    assert second_body["next_run"] == first_body["next_run"]

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated premium-tier customer exists with tier=premium
Source account has sufficient funds
Frequency value HOURLY is not supported by the system
A unique Idempotency-Key has been generated for this request
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with body {amount:1000, currency:USD, frequency:HOURLY, source_account:<id>, destination_account:<id>} and headers Authorization: Bearer <premium_token> and Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Capture HTTP response status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an error or detail field referencing unsupported frequency
trace: R-FIN-080: error pathway - 422 when frequency unsupported
R-FIN-080: triggers - customer schedules transfer with an invalid frequency
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(api_client, premium_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "HOURLY",
        "source_account": "<source_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<destination_account_id>"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
